{"problem_standardized_math":"Let\n\\[\n\\psi(t,\\sigma;\\mu)=\n\\begin{cases}\n-\\sigma t+\\dfrac{\\mu}{2}t^2, & \\text{if } t-\\sigma/\\mu\\le 0,\\\\[4pt]\n-\\dfrac{1}{2\\mu}\\sigma^2, & \\text{otherwise}.\n\\end{cases}\n\\]\nShow that the second derivative of \\(\\psi(t,\\sigma;\\mu)\\) with respect to \\(t\\) is discontinuous at \\(t=\\sigma/\\mu\\). Assuming that \\(c_i:\\mathbf{R}^n\\to\\mathbf{R}\\) is twice continuously differentiable, write down the matrix of second partial derivatives with respect to \\(x\\) of \\(\\psi(c_i(x),\\lambda_i;\\mu)\\) in the two cases \\(c_i(x)<\\lambda_i/\\mu\\) and \\(c_i(x)\\ge \\lambda_i/\\mu\\)."}
